pi_items.source: Task mandate setup script (setup_task.sh) and context
Task mandate and Panoply launcher script (/workspace/tasks/desalination_membrane_thermal_stress/setup_task.sh)
OPENDAP inspection of NOAA OI SST v2 long-term mean dataset via netCDF4
Task mandate instructions and export script paths
Task mandate expectations and export_result.sh checks

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real NOAA OI SST v2 long-term mean data (1991-2020) downloaded from PSL NOAA. Using an independent python OPENDAP netCDF4 script, actual long-term mean sea surface temperatures around Egypt confirm that the verifier correctly bounds realistic values: the Mediterranean coast averages ~17.0°C in February and ~27.1°C in August, while the Red Sea averages ~22.5°C in February and ~29.2°C in August, properly classifying the Red Sea as the highest peak thermal stress environment.
